title: Set up Sentry in a Next.js project
summary: Complete Sentry integration for Next.js — wizard install, config files, source maps, error boundaries, and performance monitoring.
declaration: I will integrate Sentry into this Next.js project using the official @sentry/nextjs SDK. This covers error tracking, performance monitoring, and source maps. If you prefer a different error monitoring service (Bugsnag, Datadog, etc.), say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set up Sentry in Next.js ### 1. Run the Sentry wizard - Run `npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs`. - The wizard will create `sentry.client.config.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `sentry.edge.config.ts`, and wrap `next.config.js`. - If the wizard fails or you prefer manual setup, proceed with step 2. ### 2. Manual install (if wizard skipped) - Install: `npm install @sentry/nextjs`. - Create `sentry.client.config.ts` at project root: ``` import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, tracesSampleRate: 1.0, replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, }); ``` - Create `sentry.server.config.ts` at project root: ``` import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, tracesSampleRate: 1.0, }); ``` - Create `sentry.edge.config.ts` at project root: ``` import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, tracesSampleRate: 1.0, }); ``` ### 3. Wrap next.config.js - Import `withSentryConfig` from `@sentry/nextjs`. - Wrap the existing config: `export default withSentryConfig(nextConfig, { org: '', project: '', silent: !process.env.CI })`. - If using `next.config.mjs` or `next.config.ts`, adjust the import syntax accordingly. ### 4. Environment variables - Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` — the client-side DSN (safe to expose). - Add `SENTRY_DSN` — the server-side DSN (same value, but server-only). - Add `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — required for source map uploads. Generate at Settings > Auth Tokens in Sentry. - Add `SENTRY_ORG` and `SENTRY_PROJECT` if not hardcoded in `next.config`. - Never commit these values. Use `.env.local` for local dev and your hosting provider's env config for production. ### 5. Source maps - Source maps upload automatically during `next build` when `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is set. - Verify upload: check Sentry dashboard under Settings > Source Maps. - If maps don't appear, ensure `productionBrowserSourceMaps` is NOT set to `false` in `next.config`. - Add `sentry.properties` to `.gitignore` if it contains tokens. ### 6. Error boundary (App Router) - Create or update `app/global-error.tsx`: ``` 'use client'; import * as Sentry from '@sentry/nextjs'; import { useEffect } from 'react'; export default function GlobalError({ error, reset }) { useEffect(() => { Sentry.captureException(error); }, [error]); return (

Something went wrong

); } ``` - For Pages Router, use `_error.tsx` with `Sentry.captureUnderscoreErrorException`. ### 7. Performance monitoring - `tracesSampleRate: 1.0` captures 100% of transactions. Lower to `0.1`–`0.2` in production to control costs. - Automatic instrumentation covers: page loads, navigations, API routes, and `getServerSideProps`. - For custom spans: `Sentry.startSpan({ name: 'my-operation' }, () => { ... })`. ### 8. Verify integration - Add a test button or API route that throws: `throw new Error('Sentry test error')`. - Check Sentry dashboard within 2 minutes for the error. - If nothing appears: check DSN is correct, check browser console for Sentry init errors, verify no ad-blocker is blocking the Sentry endpoint. ### Common errors & fixes - **'Cannot find module @sentry/nextjs'** → Run `npm install @sentry/nextjs` again. Check you're not accidentally importing from `@sentry/react` or `@sentry/node`. - **Source maps not uploading** → Ensure `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` is set in the build environment, not just `.env.local`. - **'Sentry reported a 401'** → Auth token is invalid or expired. Regenerate at Sentry > Settings > Auth Tokens. - **Build time doubled** → Source map upload is slow on large projects. Add `silent: true` to suppress logs, or set `disableServerWebpackPlugin: true` during dev. - **'window is not defined'** → You imported Sentry client config in a server component. Keep client and server configs separate. - **Duplicate errors flooding dashboard** → Set `beforeSend` to deduplicate or filter noisy errors. Adjust `sampleRate` for non-critical errors. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT put `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` in client-side code or `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefixed vars — it's a secret. - Do NOT set `tracesSampleRate: 1.0` in production without understanding the cost — Sentry bills by event volume. - Do NOT wrap every component in `Sentry.ErrorBoundary` — use the global error boundary and add granular ones only where needed. - Do NOT ignore the Sentry wizard output — it configures the webpack plugin and instrumentation files correctly. - Do NOT install `@sentry/react` and `@sentry/node` separately — `@sentry/nextjs` bundles both and handles the correct import per environment. - Do NOT commit `sentry.properties` or `.sentryclirc` if they contain auth tokens.
